product_goal_summary: Baton Flow produces trustworthy outputs across AI-assisted sessions. Tasks carry context (batons) across handoffs so reports â€” the valuable output â€” accumulate quality under human steering. The system kills context loss, opacity, and steering inability between AI sessions.
architecture_intent: A single-store, CLI-gated, projection-derived architecture. One SQLite database holds all truth; markdown projections are derived product surface (never canonical). The CLI is the sole write path. Five invariants (dependency, identity, ownership, lifecycle, existence) constrain the engine; everything above that floor is agent judgment. The architecture separates the means (baton/context) from the output (report) and makes each independently specifiable.

source_of_truth_map.escalation_state: SQLite database, per-escalation rows with stable IDs (HLD-005)
source_of_truth_map.markdown_projections: Derived from store â€” product surface, never canonical (HLD-003)
source_of_truth_map.invariant_discipline: flow.py FlowError tags â€” the closed set (HLD-015)

ownership_boundaries.lifecycle_layer: Owns state machine transitions and dependency guards (FLOW-F02)
ownership_boundaries.routing_layer: Owns session enforcement, affinity binding, verb dispatch (FLOW-F03)
ownership_boundaries.fork_join_layer: Owns escalation/split primitives and wake semantics (FLOW-F04)
ownership_boundaries.context_layer: Owns baton read/write contract, append-log, multi-writer (FLOW-F06)
ownership_boundaries.output_layer: Owns outcome/report distinction, report lifecycle, supersession (FLOW-F08)

contracts_and_seams: CLI entry gate: session enforcement (all verbs, reads included) â€” seam between external callers and internal state
Transaction boundary: every verb = one BEGIN IMMEDIATEâ€¦COMMIT â€” seam between CLI and store
Projection derivation: store commits â†’ projection re-derives â€” seam between canonical and derived
Invariant boundary: five tagged invariants separate engine constraints from agent judgment
Fork-join contract: escalation ID stability, per-escalation resolution, all-resolved wake
Ownership fence: hold-by-session check at done/escalate/split â€” seam between concurrent sessions
Report lifecycle contract: activeâ†’deprecated boundary, immutability, reference forwarding
brownfield_constraints: Existing flow.py implements a subset: single reply verb (not answer/feedback split), optional sessions (not mandatory), single escalation slot (not concurrent), no report layer
HLD-017 records design-vs-implementation gap â€” the design is authoritative, the gap is implementation status
Existing test_flow.py has 66 passing tests that must not regress
SQLite database schema already exists with tasks, batons â€” schema migration needed for multi-escalation and reports
core.md loop document already defines the runner contract â€” spec must align with existing runner behavior where it matches the HLD

expert_lenses_applied.software_architecture: Single-store architecture with derived projections eliminates split-brain. Five-invariant boundary is a clean separation of concerns between engine constraints and agent policy. The layered decomposition (storeâ†’lifecycleâ†’routingâ†’fork-joinâ†’answerâ†’contextâ†’recoveryâ†’output) respects dependency order.
expert_lenses_applied.brownfield_integration: Implementation extends existing flow.py (66 tests, single file). Migration path: concurrent escalations require schema extension; answer/feedback replace reply; reports are net-new. Each feature is specifiable against the existing codebase with clear test expectations.
expert_lenses_applied.contracts_and_seams: Seven contracts identified above. The critical one is the CLI-entry session gate â€” it establishes identity before any operation. The transaction boundary (one tx per verb) is the crash-safety seam. The invariant boundary separates what the engine enforces from what agents decide.
expert_lenses_applied.ai_agent_toolchain_safety: Agents operate through CLI only (no direct DB). The identity invariant means every action is attributable. The ownership fence prevents session-theft. The autonomy contract (HLD-015) explicitly delegates judgment to agents within invariant bounds â€” agents cannot violate invariants but can make any judgment call above them.
expert_lenses_applied.test_and_evidence: Each feature has a named verification path. Store: crash/concurrency tests. Lifecycle: state-transition coverage + tagging audit. Sessions: enforcement tests. Fork-join: multi-escalation tests. Answer/feedback: routing tests. Baton: multi-writer tests. Recovery: lease/fence tests. Output: lifecycle/immutability tests.
expert_lenses_applied.git_worktree_pr_lifecycle: 8 features = 8 reviewable units. Dependency order gives a natural PR sequence. Each feature is testable in isolation (mocking only upstream features that are not yet implemented in the same branch).
expert_lenses_applied.slice_quality: Each feature is one purpose, one verification path, one stable checkpoint. No feature requires whole-system context â€” each can be specified and tested against its declared dependencies only.

domain_assumptions: ASSUMPTION-1: SQLite is the store (HLD-012 implementation detail). The spec treats it as given brownfield reality, not a decision to make.
ASSUMPTION-2: Python 3.10+ (HLD-012). Brownfield constraint, not a choice.
ASSUMPTION-3: AI runners (Claude, Devin, Codex) are the primary runners (HLD-012). The CLI contract is runner-agnostic by design.
ASSUMPTION-4: The existing flow.py single-file architecture continues â€” specs add to it rather than restructuring into multiple modules.
ASSUMPTION-5: Reports are net-new (not yet implemented). The spec defines them from scratch within the existing architecture.

slice_roadmap.name: Store & Transaction Foundation
slice_roadmap.purpose: Establish the single-store contract, transaction guarantees, and projection derivation boundary as the foundation everything else builds on.
required_tests: Concurrent flow next claims each task at most once
Crash mid-operation leaves no partial state
Projection re-derivable after delete
slice_roadmap.rollback_story: Store contract is foundational â€” rollback means reverting the spec, not the code (existing code already implements most of this).
slice_roadmap.architecture_value: Establishes the single-source-of-truth property that every other feature depends on.

slice_roadmap.name: Task Lifecycle & Invariant Discipline
slice_roadmap.purpose: Define the four-state machine and the closed invariant set that bounds what the engine may constrain.
required_tests: Four states only (CHECK constraint)
Dependency guard blocks done with open deps
Every FlowError tagged from closed set
Untaggable guard flagged
slice_roadmap.rollback_story: Revert spec to existing lifecycle behavior (already implemented). The invariant-tagging audit is additive and independently revertible.
slice_roadmap.architecture_value: Establishes the state machine and the boundary between engine constraints and agent judgment.

slice_roadmap.name: Named Sessions, CLI Entry & Work Routing
slice_roadmap.purpose: Define mandatory session enforcement and soft-affinity routing as the identity and dispatch layer.
expected_files_or_areas: flow.py (CLI entry, session check, next routing)
required_tests: Missing session rejected on all verbs including reads
First labeled claim binds session
Fallback to any when no label-match
None only when no runnable work
slice_roadmap.rollback_story: Session enforcement is a gate â€” revert to optional sessions (existing behavior) by removing the entry check. Routing is additive.
slice_roadmap.architecture_value: Establishes identity (no anonymous path) and work distribution. Prerequisite for ownership fence and recovery.

slice_roadmap.name: Fork-Join & Concurrent Escalations
slice_roadmap.purpose: Define the unified fork-join primitive with concurrent escalation support.
required_tests: Multiple escalations coexist on one task
Per-escalation resolution independent
Task wakes only when ALL resolved
Split creates children and parks parent
slice_roadmap.rollback_story: Revert to single-escalation slot (existing behavior). Multi-escalation is a schema extension â€” revert = drop the extension.
slice_roadmap.architecture_value: Establishes the fork-join primitive that answer/feedback and recovery both depend on.

slice_roadmap.name: Human-in-the-Loop â€” Answer & Feedback
slice_roadmap.purpose: Define the answer/feedback discrimination that replaces the overloaded v1 reply verb.
allowed_changes: Answer verb spec (routes to specific escalation)
required_tests: Answer resolves specific escalation by ID
Answer rejected when no matching open escalation
Feedback on report defers to agent judgment
Feedback as new scope creates referenced task
slice_roadmap.rollback_story: Revert to single reply verb (existing behavior). Answer/feedback is a verb split â€” revert = keep reply.
slice_roadmap.architecture_value: Establishes the humanâ†’system channel discrimination. Prerequisite for report feedback (F08).

slice_roadmap.name: Baton â€” Context Substrate
slice_roadmap.purpose: Define the per-task context blackboard, its append-log semantics, and its distinction from outcome/report.
required_tests: Claimed-by recorded on baton within claim transaction
note/decide are multi-writer (no fence)
Baton is distinct from outcome (test both exist on done)
Projection derivable from DB baton
slice_roadmap.rollback_story: Baton is already implemented â€” spec codifies existing behavior. Rollback = revert spec only.
slice_roadmap.architecture_value: Establishes the context mechanism. Prerequisite for outcome (F08) since outcome is distinct from baton.

slice_roadmap.name: Recovery â€” Lease, Reclaim, Fence, Flaky-mark
slice_roadmap.purpose: Define liveness recovery without corrupting state: lease, reclaim, fence, and permanent flaky-mark.
expected_files_or_areas: flow.py (reclaim, lease refresh, fence checks, flaky_mark)
required_tests: Lease-expired task reclaimed in flow next
Explicit reclaim returns to pending immediately
Fence rejects done/escalate/split by non-holder
At RECLAIM_MAX further orphaning escalates with scoped escalation
slice_roadmap.rollback_story: Revert to lease-only reclaim (existing behavior). Explicit reclaim verb and flaky-mark are additive.
slice_roadmap.architecture_value: Establishes liveness guarantees without weakening correctness invariants.

slice_roadmap.name: Output Layer â€” Outcome & Report
slice_roadmap.purpose: Define the three-level output stack: mandatory outcome at done, reports as transcendent deliverables with independent lifecycle.
forbidden_changes: Answer/feedback routing (that is F05, though feedback triggers updates here)
required_tests: done requires outcome (mandatory)
Long outcome is still outcome not report
Report lifecycle transitions
Deprecated report is immutable
References resolve to live successor
Every update attributed
slice_roadmap.rollback_story: Reports are net-new â€” rollback = don't add them. Mandatory outcome is a tightening of done â€” rollback = keep optional.
slice_roadmap.architecture_value: Establishes the output layer that is the system's reason to exist â€” separating context (baton) from result (outcome) from deliverable (report).

next_slice_packet.name: Store & Transaction Foundation
next_slice_packet.reason_next: Root of the dependency graph â€” no feature can be specified without first establishing the single-store contract and transaction semantics. Also the lowest-risk starting point: existing code already implements most of this; the spec codifies proven behavior.
next_slice_packet.entry_point: Specify the SQLite store contract, WAL settings, one-transaction-per-verb rule, and projection-after-commit boundary against existing flow.py implementation.
next_slice_packet.stop_condition: Store contract specified with all three HLD-013 verification points testable.

test_strategy: Each feature carries required_tests (specific, named). Tests prove invariants, not implementation details. Cross-feature integration tested bottom-up: each slice assumes its dependencies pass. Final integration: all 8 features compose without regression against the existing 66-test suite.
forbidden_shortcuts: TRAP: Implementing reports before the baton/outcome distinction is specified â€” risks conflating context with output.
TRAP: Making sessions optional 'for now' â€” the identity invariant (HLD-015) requires mandatory sessions from the start.
TRAP: Single-escalation slot 'extended later' â€” concurrent escalations are a schema decision that must be in the first fork-join spec.
TRAP: Treating projections as source of truth â€” every test must prove re-derivability.
TRAP: Skipping the overreach-detection audit â€” without it, new guards accumulate silently outside the invariant boundary.
growth_and_change_notes: The architecture absorbs growth through: (1) new CLI verbs add behind the same session-enforcement gate; (2) new state or entities add to the single store; (3) the invariant boundary is closed â€” new constraints must justify tagging or be rejected as overreach. The candidate capabilities in HLD-017 (Web UI, HTTP API, etc.) would add interfaces above the CLI but never below the invariant floor â€” the boundary rule (HLD-011) governs their adoption.

helper_recommendation.rationale: Single-file Python target with clear brownfield constraints, well-defined feature boundaries, and strong dependency ordering. SpecKit's constitution-backed spec workflow matches the invariant-driven architecture. Alternative: claude-code for rapid iteration on the existing flow.py.
helper_recommendation.registry_note: Advisory only â€” helper selection is a Journey 3 decision.
